title: Supplementary Information for: Transcriptomic Analysis Reveals Phenotype-Dependent Ferroptosis and Stress Responses Induced by Selenomethylselenocystine via the NRF2–ATF4 Axis in Pancreatic Cancer Cells
author: SELVAM, Arun Kumar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset contains supplementary information associated with the manuscript:  "Transcriptomic Analysis Reveals Phenotype-Dependent Ferroptosis and Stress Responses Induced by Selenomethylselenocystine via the NRF2–ATF4 Axis in Pancreatic Cancer Cells"  by Mehran Ghaderi¹˒²† , Joakim Dillner¹˒², Mikael Björnstedt²˒³, and Arun Kumar Selvam³† . 
 The supplementary file includes additional figures, tables, and extended methods referenced in the main manuscript. These materials support the interpretation of RNA-seq analyses, ferroptosis markers, stress pathway signatures, and cell viability assessments across pancreatic cancer models. 
   Supplementary Table   
   Supplementary Table 1:    Oligonucleotides used  
   Supplementary Table 2:    PCR amplification conditions  
   Supplementary Table 3:    List of qPCR primers used for validation in this study  
   Supplementary Figure   
   Supplementary Figure 1. Principal component analysis (PCA) of MSC-treated pancreatic cancer cell lines.    PCA plots showing the transcriptomic response of CAPAN-2 cells at 8 h of MSC treatment for ( A ) 100 µM and ( B ) 250 µM.   
   Supplementary Figure 2. Sample-to-sample distance heatmap of CAPAN-2 and PANC-1 cell lines treated with MSC.    A Euclidean distance-based heatmap visualizing sample-to-sample relationships in ( A–D ) CAPAN-2 cells treated with 100 µM and 250 mM of MSC for 8 and 20 hours. Control and MSC-treated samples form clearly distinct clusters, with tight intra-group similarity, and ( E–F ) in PANC-1 cells, treated with 100 µM and 250 mM of MSC for 20 hours. This supports a consistent and reproducible transcriptional response to MSC treatment and aligns with the PCA results shown in Figure 1. Each sample represents one biological replicate. Color intensity reflects the distance metric, with darker colors indicating greater dissimilarity between samples.  
   Supplementary Figure 3.     DEGs shared across MSC 100 µM and MSC 250 µM at 20 h treatments in  ( A ) CAPAN-2 and ( B ) PANC-1 cell lines. DEGs were defined using an adjusted p-value < 0.01.  
   Supplementary Files:   
   Supplementary File 1:    List of differentially expressed genes (DEGs) in CAPAN-2, PANC-1 and HPAF-II cells under all MSC treatment conditions.   
   Supplementary File 2:    Gene Ontology (GO) enrichment analysis results for CAPAN-2 and PANC-1 DEGs under different MSC treatment conditions.
